Brilliant Recalls Launch PLC 1
Now I want to look at Susan Garrett's launch.
!
Susan Garret is a dog agility trainer, the launch is for her online course, called
BrilliantRecalls.com. Dog agility, it's a little niche within the dog training market which of
course is part of the dog market which is part of the pet market.
!
Susan's list is under 20,000 people but to my knowledge, this is one of the most
successful internal launches ever. Susan likes to keep her numbers private so I don't
want to give you the numbers. They were shocking. I just have to say that they were
absolutely shocking, the results she got from this launch. You definitely want to pay
attention to this.
!
Susan joined Product Launch Formula in 2008. Before that, she had no list. In her
words, she relied entirely on hope marketing. She did have a few DVDs that she would
sell through distributors. She has articles that were going on journals. She's very, very
highly accomplished as a dog trainer with national titles and world titles in that very
small dog agility market.
She has a dog training business where she actually trains other dog trainers. She had to
travel to events just all year round, with tons and tons of travel. When she joined
Product Launch Formula, she had no idea about this world, this information marketing
world, this online education world. She didn't know about list building but she quickly
built a list of best clients.
It was in the very first month at Product Launch Formula she did a launch that did
$27,000. That was just the start of her journey. In the next year after Product Launch
Formula, her business grew by a factor of 6.4. In other words, it grew 640% in the one
year after Product Launch Formula when she started putting it to use and doing
launches in her business and focusing and building her list.
!
The year after that, it grew another 2.5 times. That means after two years, it was 16
times bigger than her business was, her dog training business was before Product
Launch Formula. In the next year after that, she grew another 4.7 times. The total
growth was 77 times the size than her business was before Product Launch Formula.
She has been able to cut her travel down so she only travels to about four or five
events per year. Again, full disclosure. Susan is in my Platinum program. She has been

She starts to tell a story. Stories can be very, very powerful. The
thing you have to remember about a story is you don't want it to 	be
overly long. You want to give enough details so it feels very real, 	it
draws people in but it's like telling a joke. You need to give 	enough
details so that the punch line makes sense. It's the same 	thing with
her stories. You definitely want to use stories. They are 	very, very
powerful but they have to make a point and you have to 	do it without
wandering around too much. Here, you'll see she tells 	a story of
being out with her dog.
!
Susan Garrett: 	Last fall, I was out taking a walk with a friend of mine. We both

She asked me to take a hold of her dog. She puts her dog on leash
and she makes her way across the field. Meanwhile my dog, Feature, is
off leash and she decides to follow her. Feature's 	prancing across the
field behind her. Now, this animal was probably 	300 meters, three
agility fields away. At this point, my friend shouts 	back, "It's just a
stray cat." Her voice gets the attention of both Feature and the cat.
Feature looks back at my friend, that cat takes off in the direction
of Feature. Basically, this cat runs full tilt right under Feature's nose
and into a group of trees. Feature's response? She stood, watched 	the
cat go by, turned around and trotted back to me without even 	giving
that cat a second thought. Now this is an example of a dog 	whose
responses have been inspired so that in the heat of the 	moment, no
decision has to be made.
